# Regression assumes causality

## Functions






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
generate_data <- function(n, population_model) {
  
  data <- lavaan::simulateData(model = population_model, sample.nobs = n) 
  
  return(data)
}

analyse <- function(data, model) {
  
  # specify and fit model
  fit <- sem(model = model, data = data)
  #fit <- lm(formula = mode, data = data)
  
  # extract regression beta estimates 
  results <- parameters::model_parameters(fit, standardize = FALSE) |>
    filter(To == "Y" & From == "X") |>  # this corresponds to the Y ~ X effect
    select(beta = Coefficient,
           ci_lower = CI_low,
           ci_upper = CI_high,
           p) 
  
  return(results)
}

 plots_causal <- function(model){
  generate_data(n = 300, population_model = model) %>%
    sem(model = model, data = .) |>
    semPaths(whatLabels = "diagram", 
             layout = layout_matrix, 
             residuals = FALSE,
             edge.label.cex = 1.2, 
             sizeMan = 10)
}
```
:::






## Does regression assume causality?

### Plots

Simple regression: X causes Y






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# simple regression
layout_matrix <- matrix(c( 1,  0,
                           -1,  0), 
                        ncol = 2, 
                        byrow = TRUE)

plots_causal("Y ~ 0.5*X")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](13_causality_files/figure-pdf/unnamed-chunk-3-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::






Simple regression: Y causes X






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# simple regression
layout_matrix <- matrix(c(-1,  0,
                           1,  0), 
                        ncol = 2, 
                        byrow = TRUE)

plots_causal("X ~ 0.5*Y")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](13_causality_files/figure-pdf/unnamed-chunk-4-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::






### Run simulation






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
experiment_parameters_grid <- expand_grid(
  n = 200,
  population_model = c("Y ~ 0.5*X",
                       "X ~ 0.5*Y"),
  analyse_model = "Y ~ X",
  iteration = 1:1000
) 

set.seed(42)

simulation <- 
  # using the experiment parameters...
  experiment_parameters_grid |>
  
  # ...generate data... 
  mutate(generated_data = pmap(list(n = n,
                                    population_model = population_model),
                               generate_data)) |>
  # ...analyze data 
  mutate(results = pmap(list(data = generated_data,
                             model = analyse_model),
                        analyse))
```
:::






### Summarize results






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
simulation_summary <- simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  group_by(n,
           population_model,
           analyse_model) |>
  summarize(mean_beta = mean(beta),
            proportion_signficiant = mean(p < .05))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'n', 'population_model'. You can override
using the `.groups` argument.
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
simulation_summary |>
  mutate_if(is.numeric, janitor::round_half_up, digits = 2) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`mutate_if()` ignored the following grouping variables:
* Columns `n`, `population_model`
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}

\begin{longtable}[t]{rllrr}
\toprule
n & population\_model & analyse\_model & mean\_beta & proportion\_signficiant\\
\midrule
200 & X \textasciitilde{} 0.5*Y & Y \textasciitilde{} X & 0.4 & 1\\
200 & Y \textasciitilde{} 0.5*X & Y \textasciitilde{} X & 0.5 & 1\\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}


:::
:::






## Collider

### Plots

Covariate is a confounder:






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
layout_matrix <- matrix(c( 1,  0,
                           -1,  0,
                           0,  1), 
                        ncol = 2, 
                        byrow = TRUE)

plots_causal("Y ~ 0.0*X + 0.5*C; X ~ 0.5*C")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](13_causality_files/figure-pdf/unnamed-chunk-7-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::






Confounder is a collider:






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
layout_matrix <- matrix(c( 0,  1,
                           1,  0,
                           -1,  0), 
                        ncol = 2, 
                        byrow = TRUE)

plots_causal("C ~ 0.5*X + 0.5*Y; Y ~ 0.0*X")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](13_causality_files/figure-pdf/unnamed-chunk-8-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::






### Run simulation






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
experiment_parameters_grid <- expand_grid(
  n = 200,
  population_model = c("Y ~ 0.5*X + 0.0*C",
                       "C ~ 0.5*X + 0.5*Y; Y ~~ 0.0*X"),
  analyse_model = "Y ~ X + C",
  iteration = 1:1000
)

set.seed(42)

simulation <- 
  # using the experiment parameters...
  experiment_parameters_grid |>
  
  # ...generate data...
  mutate(generated_data = pmap(list(n = n,
                                    population_model = population_model),
                               generate_data)) |>
  # ...analyze data
  mutate(results = pmap(list(data = generated_data,
                             model = analyse_model),
                        analyse))
```
:::






### Summarize results






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
simulation_summary <- simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  group_by(n,
           population_model,
           analyse_model) |>
  summarize(mean_beta = mean(beta),
            proportion_signficiant = mean(p < .05))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'n', 'population_model'. You can override
using the `.groups` argument.
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
simulation_summary |>
  mutate_if(is.numeric, janitor::round_half_up, digits = 2) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`mutate_if()` ignored the following grouping variables:
* Columns `n`, `population_model`
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}

\begin{longtable}[t]{rllrr}
\toprule
n & population\_model & analyse\_model & mean\_beta & proportion\_signficiant\\
\midrule
200 & C \textasciitilde{} 0.5*X + 0.5*Y; Y \textasciitilde{}\textasciitilde{} 0.0*X & Y \textasciitilde{} X + C & -0.2 & 0.82\\
200 & Y \textasciitilde{} 0.5*X + 0.0*C & Y \textasciitilde{} X + C & 0.5 & 1.00\\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}


:::
